refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability KPI is decomposed to focus on minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery, which are key contributors to availability. This allows targeted design and implementation decisions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual operational uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Minimize unplanned downtime of the NGHE solution
Enable rapid recovery from faults or failures

candidate_dps: Redundant system components and fault detection to minimize downtime
Automated fault recovery and restart procedures to enable rapid recovery
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant system components with fault detection and automated recovery procedures to minimize downtime and enable rapid recovery

potential_sources: Fault detection logic tightly coupled with recovery procedures
Redundancy mechanisms interacting with multiple subsystems
coupling_check.notes: The DP logically groups fault detection and recovery to reduce coupling with other unrelated functions, maintaining modularity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes fault detection and recovery can be effectively integrated within the NGHE solution architecture
Risk that recovery procedures may require coordination across multiple modules, increasing complexity
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor integration complexity during implementation; consider further decomposition if coupling increases.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for availability including redundancy, fault detection, and recovery

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to monitor system health and report status for fault detection and recovery coordination

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands that may affect availability states

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts related to faults impacting safety and availability

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication for remote monitoring and teleoperation impacting availability

module_containment_scores.notes: Core availability logic and recovery procedures are best contained here to minimize diffusion.
Vehicle hardware contributes to availability but recovery logic is less suited here.
Teleoperation affects availability indirectly; containing availability logic here would increase diffusion.
Safety alerts interface with availability but core logic should remain in NGHE Solution.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing availability logic and recovery in NGHE Solution module reduces diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution module due to integration of multiple availability-related functions
Coordination with Vehicle and Safety Services modules may require well-defined interfaces
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and monitor module complexity during development.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by addressing both minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if recovery procedures require cross-module coordination, but containment scores support current assignment.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment in NGHE Solution module is appropriate given high containment score and low diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability can be verified via telemetry data and operational logs collected through the NGHE Solution interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of availability responsibility to NGHE Solution module is clear and aligns with system ownership.

recommended_revisions: Monitor integration complexity within NGHE Solution module.
Ensure interface definitions with Vehicle and Safety Services are robust.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and meet the acceptance criteria with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize unplanned downtime of the NGHE solution
Enable rapid recovery from faults or failures

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Fault detection and recovery can be integrated within NGHE Solution module.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Safety Services will be well-defined.
open_risks: Complexity of recovery coordination across modules.
evidence_needed: Operational availability data from telemetry during evaluation period.
Verification of fault detection and recovery effectiveness.
